title: compact_with_hint with all-preserved messages: error 'did not reduce token count' when categorize yields zero summarizable
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/compaction.rs:436-546 (compact_with_hint), interplay with categorize_messages at 389-412
## Violation
`compact_with_hint` returns `Err(CompactionError::Failed("Compaction did not reduce token count"))` whenever the categorization decides every message must be preserved. This happens routinely when:

- `preserve_recent` covers all messages (small conversations near the threshold).
- `preserve_system + preserve_tool_calls` cover every message (typical agent transcript: system prompt + interleaved tool calls).

```rust
if new_tokens >= analysis.current_tokens {
    warn!(...);
    return Err(CompactionError::Failed("Compaction did not reduce token count".to_string()));
}
```

The caller has no way to distinguish "we cannot compact further, send the request anyway" from "compaction blew up, abort". Today both surfaces as a hard Err. Worse, before that Err returns, `request.messages = new_messages` (line 513) has already mutated the request — the caller sees a mutated request AND an error. (#439 already covers the rollback aspect for the post-verify path; this issue is the orthogonal "no work was possible" outcome.)
## Mandated refactor
1. Add a third variant to `CompactionResult`: `compacted: bool, reason: CompactionSkipReason` where reason ∈ {NotNeeded, NothingToSummarize, AllPreserved}.
2. When `messages_to_summarize.is_empty()` (line 493) OR the post-rebuild check shows no reduction, return Ok with the appropriate reason, not Err.
3. Reserve `CompactionError::Failed` for actual logic failures (summary generation panicked, hook misbehaved).
4. Snapshot `request.messages` at function entry and restore it before returning the skip Ok, mirroring the rollback discipline #439 mandates for the verify-failure path.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
